compiled_state.title: Advanced bloat pattern detection (semantic and cross-file analysis)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Five bloat patterns require semantic understanding, cross-file graph analysis, or data flow tracking that exceeds what static analysis tools can reliably provide. These patterns were identified during brainstorm for the foundation remediation epic (w21-bsnz) and deferred because no available detection engine (semgrep, ast-grep, bash-grep, per-stack linters) can achieve sufficient confidence for automated or blue-team-routed detection. This epic extends the /dso:remediate skill and confidence mapping infrastructure established by w21-bsnz.

## Success Criteria
- P-D4 (concept drift across doc sections): Detects contradictory statements within the same document using NLP semantic analysis
- P-D1 (orphaned sections with no cross-references): Builds a cross-document reference graph and identifies sections with zero inbound references
- P-T2 (mock-heavy test with no behavioral assertion): Detects tests with high mock-to-assertion ratios using data flow analysis to distinguish mock verification from behavioral assertion
- P-C2 (parameter always called with same literal at all call sites): Aggregates call sites across files to detect parameters that are always passed the same value
- P-C1 upgrade (environment-aware feature flag detection): Extends P-C1 from w21-bsnz to handle environment-variable-gated flags that are statically never true but dynamically enabled in production

## Dependencies
- w21-bsnz (Create a process to remediate legacy code): Establishes the confidence mapping YAML schema, routing infrastructure, blue-team agent, and resolver agent that this epic extends

## Approach
Needs /dso:brainstorm before implementation — patterns require novel detection approaches not yet established. Research directions: fine-tuned CodeBERT for semantic dead code (see DCE-LLM, NAACL 2025), mutation testing as ground truth for T2 (see RTj), NLP contradiction detection for D4.
